auth.title: API Key Required
auth.description: This instance requires an API key. Enter the key from your config.toml.
auth.hint: Add api_key = "your-key" at the top of ~/.librefang/config.toml

GeneralAssistant.name: General Assistant
GeneralAssistant.desc: A versatile conversational agent that can help with everyday tasks, answer questions, and provide recommendations.

CodeHelper.name: Code Helper
CodeHelper.desc: A programming-focused agent that writes, reviews, and debugs code across multiple languages.

Researcher.name: Researcher
Researcher.desc: An analytical agent that breaks down complex topics, synthesizes information, and provides cited summaries.

Writer.name: Writer
Writer.desc: A creative writing agent that helps with drafting, editing, and improving written content of all kinds.

DataAnalyst.name: Data Analyst
DataAnalyst.desc: A data-focused agent that helps analyze datasets, create queries, and interpret statistical results.

DevOpsEngineer.name: DevOps Engineer
DevOpsEngineer.desc: A systems-focused agent for CI/CD, infrastructure, Docker, and deployment troubleshooting.

CustomerSupport.name: Customer Support
CustomerSupport.desc: A professional, empathetic agent for handling customer inquiries and resolving issues.

Tutor.name: Tutor
Tutor.desc: A patient educational agent that explains concepts step-by-step and adapts to the learner's level.

APIDesigner.name: API Designer
APIDesigner.desc: An agent specialized in RESTful API design, OpenAPI specs, and integration architecture.

MeetingNotes.name: Meeting Notes
MeetingNotes.desc: Summarizes meeting transcripts into structured notes with action items and key decisions.

overview.welcomeDesc: Get started quickly with the guided Setup Wizard, or configure manually:
overview.activityWillAppear: Activity will appear here once agents start processing.

agentChat.selectAgentToChat: Select an agent to start chatting
agentChat.chooseAgentFromSidebar: Choose an agent from the sidebar or go to the Agents tab to create a new one.
agentChat.availableModels: Available models - pick one or keep typing
agentChat.toolFiltersDesc: Allowlist: only these tools available (empty = all). Blocklist: these tools excluded.
agentChat.stopAgentConfirm: Stop agent "{name}"? The agent will be shut down.
agentChat.welcomeTips:
**Welcome to LibreFang Chat!**

- Type `/` to see available commands
- `/help` shows all commands
- `/think on` enables extended reasoning
- `/context` shows context window usage
- `/verbose off` hides tool details
- `Ctrl+Shift+F` toggles focus mode
- Drag & drop files to attach them
- `Ctrl+/` opens the command palette

sessionsPage.title: Sessions
sessionsPage.conversationSessionsDesc: Each conversation with an agent creates a session. Sessions store the full message history so you can resume conversations later, or review past interactions.
sessionsPage.sessionsCreatedWhen: Sessions are created when you chat with agents. Start a conversation to see session history here.
sessionsPage.agentMemoryDesc: Each agent has its own key-value memory store. Agents use memory to persist preferences, notes, and context between conversations.
sessionsPage.noMemoryEntries: This agent has no memory entries yet. Agents create memory entries automatically during conversations, or you can add them manually.
sessionsPage.chooseAgentFromDropdown: Choose an agent from the dropdown above to browse and edit its memory store.
sessionsPage.deleteSessionConfirm: This will permanently remove the session and its messages.
sessionsPage.deleteKeyConfirm: Delete key "{key}"? This cannot be undone.

setupWizard.title: Setup Wizard
setupWizard.libreFangDesc: LibreFang is an open-source Agent Operating System. It lets you run AI agents that can chat, use tools, access memory, and connect to messaging channels - all from a single dashboard.
setupWizard.connectLlmProvider: Connect an LLM provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, etc.)
setupWizard.createFirstAgent: Create your first AI agent from 10 templates
setupWizard.tryWithTestMessage: Try it out with a quick test message
setupWizard.optionallyConnectChannel: Optionally connect a messaging channel (Telegram, Discord, Slack)
setupWizard.takesAbout2Min: Takes about 2 minutes. You can skip any step and configure later.
setupWizard.needsAtLeastOne: LibreFang needs at least one LLM provider to power your agents. Select a provider and enter your API key.
setupWizard.alreadyHaveProvider: You already have at least one provider set up. You can continue to the next step or configure additional providers.
setupWizard.claudeCodeNoApiKey: Claude Code uses its own CLI authentication - no API key needed.
setupWizard.claudeCodeNotDetected: Claude Code CLI not detected. Make sure you've run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code && claude auth
setupWizard.youCanTestOrContinue: You can test the connection or continue to the next step.
setupWizard.pickTemplateToStart: Pick a template to get started quickly. You can customize the agent later or create more from the Agents page.
setupWizard.sendQuickMessage: Send a quick message to test your new agent. Try one of the suggestions below or type your own.
setupWizard.channelsLetAgent: Channels let your agent communicate via messaging platforms. This is optional - you can always use the built-in web chat.
setupWizard.canSkipThis: You can skip this step. The built-in web chat is always available from the Agents page. Add channels any time from Settings -> Channels.
setupWizard.libreFangConfigured: LibreFang is configured and ready to go. Here is a summary of what was set up:
setupWizard.openAgentsToChat: Open Agents to start talking to your agent
setupWizard.goToAgentsToCreate: Go to Agents to create your first agent
setupWizard.browseSkillsToAdd: Browse Skills to add capabilities (web search, code execution, etc.)
setupWizard.visitChannelsToConnect: Visit Channels to connect messaging platforms
setupWizard.pleaseEnterAgentName: Please enter a name for your agent
